movies.title: Fireworks
movies.review: Good movie to watch if you’d like to fall asleep in a cinema. Bad movie to watch if you enjoy having an understanding of timelines.

movies.title: Coward
movies.review:
SFF #7
So fucking tender. The two main actors put on phenomenal performances and I was hooked. Hoping for a good sob like when watching Close but wasn’t quite as sad, not to say I didn’t shed a few tears.

movies.title: Summer 2000: The X-Cetra Story
movies.review:
SFF #6
Wins points for nostalgia value but too much time was spent delving into personal lives in a way that felt unimportant (and at times, unauthentic) compared to the main crux of the doco.

movies.title: Obsession
movies.review: I’m not trying to be contrarian but I genuinely found the middle chunk of this movie *incredibly* boring. I will say, the car jump scare got me real good (I yelled in the cinema) but this was a painful watch and didn’t live up to the hype.

movies.title: Everytime
movies.review:
SFF #5
A real togs, togs, undies of a storyline. It was generally working until it the last 3rd or so where it took a surrealist turn that felt out of place and completely lost me (not that I really cared very much for the film before then). After that I was simply waiting for the film to end.

merge_variables.latest_review: Good movie to watch if you’d like to fall asleep in a cinema. Bad movie to watch if you enjoy having an understanding of timelines.
